description: Please see Reaching Equilibrium . The ratio limit for mechanical calculations using the model solve command can be calculated in three ways, as defined by the following keywords:

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.description: Please see Reaching Equilibrium. The ratio limit for mechanical calculations using the model solve command can be calculated in three ways, as defined by the following keywords:

keywords.name: average
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the average unbalanced mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model divided by the average applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes (default).

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the maximum value of the ratio of the unbalanced mechanical force magnitude to the applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model. The ratio used is scaled by 0.1 to make it mean approximately the same as average.

keywords.name: maximum
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the maximum unbalanced mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model divided by the average applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes. The ratio used is scaled by 0.1 to make it mean approximately the same as average.

keywords.name: convergence
keywords.description: The ratio is defined as the maximum convergence of all the nodes in the model, with the convergence of a node defined as the local ratio divided by the target ratio. The ratio used is scaled by 1e-5 to make it mean approximately the same as average.

keywords.name: average
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the average unbalanced mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model divided by the average applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes (default).

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the maximum value of the ratio of the unbalanced mechanical force magnitude to the applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model. The ratio used is scaled by 0.1 to make it mean approximately the same as average .

keywords.name: maximum
keywords.description: The ratio is defined to be the maximum unbalanced mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes in the model divided by the average applied mechanical force magnitude for all the nodes. The ratio used is scaled by 0.1 to make it mean approximately the same as average .

keywords.name: convergence
keywords.description: The ratio is defined as the maximum convergence of all the nodes in the model, with the convergence of a node defined as the local ratio divided by the target ratio. The ratio used is scaled by 1e-5 to make it mean approximately the same as average .
